Review: Absolutely stunning views from the room, woke up to the mountains every morning.
The staff went above and beyond to make our anniversary special.
Best hotel I have stayed in India, the pool area was immaculate.
Loved the local cuisine served at the restaurant, authentic and delicious.
Room was spacious, clean and the bed was incredibly comfortable.
The location is perfect, walking distance to all major attractions.
Friendly and helpful front desk team, made check-in a breeze.
Breathtaking beach view from the balcony, we never wanted to leave.
Excellent value for money, got luxury experience at a mid-range price.
The spa was world class, best massage I have ever had.
Housekeeping was thorough and the room smelled fresh every day.
Loved the rooftop restaurant, the sunset view was magical.
The hotel arranged a local sightseeing tour for us, very thoughtful.
Kids loved the pool and the staff was very attentive to families.
Superb breakfast spread with both Indian and continental options.
The heritage property gave such a royal feel, truly unforgettable.
Warm welcome with flower garlands and traditional music, felt very special.
Perfectly located near the ghats, the evening aarti view was divine.
The houseboat experience in Alleppey was a dream come true.
Cozy mountain cottage feel with modern amenities, loved every bit.
The jungle safari arranged by the hotel was thrilling and well organized.
Staff remembered our names from day one, felt like home.
The infinity pool overlooking the valley was absolutely spectacular.
Great Wi-Fi and work desk setup, perfect for a workcation.
The hotel chef personally came to ask about dietary preferences, impressive.
Loved the cultural evening with folk dance and music arranged by the resort.
The desert camp experience was magical, stargazing in Jaisalmer was unforgettable.
Pristine beach right outside the hotel, water was crystal clear.
The Ayurvedic spa treatments were deeply relaxing and authentic.
Excellent concierge service, they arranged everything from tickets to transfers.
The room had a private plunge pool, absolute luxury at its finest.
Loved the eco-friendly practices of the resort, very responsible tourism.
The tea estate view from the room in Munnar was simply gorgeous.
Night safari was well organized and we spotted a tiger, incredible!
The hotel had a beautiful courtyard with a bonfire every evening.
Check-in was smooth and the welcome drink was refreshing.
The backwater cruise arranged by the hotel was peaceful and scenic.
Loved the rooftop yoga session with a Himalayan backdrop.
The food quality was consistently excellent throughout our five day stay.
Beautiful property with lush gardens and very attentive service.
It was an okay stay, nothing particularly stood out.
The room was decent but the view was blocked by another building.
Service was average, had to ask multiple times for towels.
The food was fine but lacked variety after the second day.
Location was convenient but the area outside was a bit noisy.
Check-in took longer than expected but the staff was polite.
The pool was clean but quite small for the number of guests.
Room was adequately clean but the decor felt a bit dated.
Breakfast was standard, nothing exciting but sufficient.
The hotel is good for a short stay but not for longer trips.
Wi-Fi was inconsistent, worked fine in the lobby but not in the room.
The gym had basic equipment, enough for a light workout.
Staff was friendly but response time for room service was slow.
The property is well maintained but lacks character.
Decent stay overall, would consider it again if the price drops.
The location is good but parking was a hassle.
Room size was smaller than what the photos suggested.
The restaurant food was average, better options available nearby.
The hotel is functional and clean but nothing memorable.
Acceptable stay for a business trip, would not choose it for leisure.
The air conditioning was noisy and disturbed our sleep.
The view from the room was partially obstructed by trees.
Service was polite but not proactive, had to ask for everything.
The property is aging and needs some renovation.
Amenities were basic but the price was fair enough.
Terrible experience, the room was dirty and smelled musty.
The staff was rude and dismissive when we raised a complaint.
Found insects in the bathroom, completely unacceptable.
The food was cold and tasteless, a huge disappointment.
We were given a room different from what we booked without any explanation.
The noise from the street made it impossible to sleep.
Hot water was not available for two days of our stay.
The hotel charged us for services we never used.
The pool was closed for maintenance but was not mentioned at booking.
Worst check-in experience ever, waited over two hours for the room.
The mattress was lumpy and the pillows were flat.
Mold on the bathroom walls, clearly not cleaned properly.
The restaurant ran out of most menu items by 8 PM.
Staff was unhelpful and gave wrong directions to local attractions.
The lift was broken for our entire stay and we were on the fifth floor.
Hidden charges appeared on the bill that were never disclosed upfront.
The room had a broken lock, felt very unsafe.
Overpriced for what was offered, much better options available nearby.
The so-called sea view room had a view of the parking lot.
Housekeeping skipped our room two days in a row.
The hotel looked nothing like the photos on the website.
Extremely slow room service, food arrived cold after 90 minutes.
The reception staff was on their phone and barely acknowledged us.
Plumbing issues throughout our stay, water pressure was very poor.
Would not recommend this hotel to anyone, complete waste of money.
